# The Redwork-Bluework Framework

Marquet's framework separates execution (redwork) from thinking (bluework).

**Case: El Faro. The captain used command-and-control language. No one challenged him. 33 died.**

**Case: USS Santa Fe. Marquet transformed the worst sub in the fleet by changing language.**

**Case: Control the Clock. Set the timing window. Let the team decide how to work within it.**

**Case: I Intend To. Language shift from seeking permission to stating intention.**

**Case: Complete the Cycle. Finish projects with reflection and recognition.**

**Case: The Santa Fe Transformation. The USS Santa Fe went from worst to best through language changes.**
**Case: The Disempowered Officer. A junior officer who waited for permission. After the shift, he took initiative.**
**Case: The Bluework Session. A scheduled time for thinking and planning improved decision quality.**
**Case: The Complete Step. After-action reviews transformed how the crew learned from mistakes.**
**Case: The Language Audit. Marquet recommends auditing your own language for command-and-control patterns.**
**Case: The Peer to Peer. The shift from leader-led to peer-based communication empowers everyone.**
**Case: The Daily Brief. A simple daily brief using the six plays prevents confusion and builds alignment.**
**Case: The Decentralized Command. Marquet's approach decentralizes decision-making to the lowest competent level.**
